technicalSummary: I work at the intersection of design and engineering, owning product problems from Figma to production code without handing off between the two.

My background is DeFi and blockchain infrastructure, which means I bring technical depth to domains most product designers approach from the outside. At Neptune Finance I built the design function from scratch: custom Figma component library, full design system, and every line of production TypeScript and Tailwind, while maintaining the engineering rigour required for live financial systems where on-chain state, wallet behaviour, and transaction reliability are non-negotiable.

I use AI tools (Cursor, Claude, and agentic workflows) as a core part of how I prototype and ship. I've directed fully agentic engineering flows, maintaining product and design ownership while accelerating implementation significantly.

coreCompetencies.description: AI tools are a core part of how I prototype and ship, from directing agentic implementation flows to integrating AI into design iteration and code review.

systemsBuilt.name: Neptune Finance Web Application
systemsBuilt.domain: DeFi lending, staking, and liquidity on Injective
systemsBuilt.outcome: Delivered a production frontend for lending, staking, airdrop, and liquidity workflows with contract-aware UX and reusable system primitives.

systemsBuilt.name: CW3 Multisig Frontend Port
systemsBuilt.outcome: Ported and refactored multisig frontend infrastructure to support Injective transaction paths and improved execution diagnostics.

systemsBuilt.name: Satsuma Cross-Chain Yield Routing
systemsBuilt.outcome: Designed and built the full frontend workflow for a cross-chain yield-to-Bitcoin conversion pipeline spanning Injective, Astroport, Axelar, and ICP. Shipped at Cosmoverse hackathon operating in a fully agentic engineering flow, directing implementation through chat while maintaining product and design ownership throughout.

projects.title: Neptune Finance
projects.roleDescriptor: Design Engineer / Frontend Lead. Full product ownership from Figma to production
projects.content: Frontend lead for Neptune Finance, a lending and borrowing system on Injective. Built the application layer that keeps contract state, wallet state, and UI state synchronized during deposits, borrows, repayments, and reward flows.
projects.content2: Implemented contract-facing features across staking, airdrop, and liquidity routing. Production flows include Mito vault integrations and Astroport pool interactions with explicit handling for transaction states, retries, and confirmation boundaries.
projects.content3: Owned the frontend system architecture in Next.js and TypeScript, including reusable UI primitives, data aggregation from chain and indexing services, and feature gating linked to on-chain staking eligibility.
scope: Full product ownership: Figma design system through production TypeScript
Custom Figma component library and visual design language
UX architecture for lending, staking, and liquidity flows
Full frontend architecture and engineering systems
Contract and wallet integration surfaces
Smart contract integrations (Mito, Astroport)
Staking, airdrop, and liquidity systems
context: Neptune Finance is a DeFi lending protocol on Injective with CosmWasm contracts.
The frontend coordinates wallet actions, contract state, and market data to keep lending and liquidity flows correct during live transaction execution.
problemSpace: On-chain and UI state can drift during pending transactions; users making financial decisions need accurate state at all times
Market-sensitive screens require low-latency updates and interfaces designed for fast decision-making
Multi-step flows across lending, staking, and liquidity must feel simple despite significant underlying contract complexity
Wallet signing and broadcast failures need explicit recovery paths; confusion at this moment breaks user trust and risks funds
approach: Model transaction lifecycle states as first-class UI states
Use reusable query and component primitives for protocol modules
Align user actions to contract message boundaries
Provide deterministic error and retry handling around wallet operations
outcomes: Shipped lending, staking, airdrop, and liquidity workflows to production users with real funds at stake
Improved transaction reliability and reduced user confusion through explicit state handling and recovery UX
Built a design system that kept Figma components and production code in sync across a fast-moving codebase
Identified and shipped the balance menu independently: no brief, designed in Figma, implemented in production

projects.title: stakefish / f2pool
projects.content: Produced technical protocol education assets for stakefish and f2pool, translating validator and staking operations into repeatable user workflows across Ethereum, Cosmos, and Solana.
projects.content2: Work prioritized procedural correctness and chain-specific detail so onboarding content matched real wallet and network steps used by operators and delegators.
projects.content3: Contributed protocol communication support during infrastructure and consensus transitions, including Ethereum's move to Proof-of-Stake.

projects.stack: Video Production, Figma, Protocol Documentation, YouTube API
scope: Created instructional visual media translating validator operations into repeatable workflows
Supported protocol updates and network transitions with developer-focused materials
Built content highlighting EVM and Cosmos ecosystem validation procedures
context: Translating technical validator node settings and staking details into visual, error-free step-by-step guides for users and operators.
Ensuring compliance with chain-specific details so onboarding content matches real-world wallet operations.
problemSpace: Technical density of staking protocols causes user mistakes during delegate/undelegate transactions
Chain-specific nuances (Ethereum vs. Solana vs. Cosmos) require highly accurate documentation
approach: Model validator onboarding flows based on real network and wallet steps
Standardize visual workflows and annotations using Figma and clear diagrams
outcomes: Shipped validator training materials for Osmosis, Evmos, Ledger, and Beacon Chain workflows
Handled official protocol messaging during Ethereum's historic transition to Proof-of-Stake

projects.title: Open Source Contributions
projects.content: Led integration work to bring CW3 Fixed Multisig support to Injective by refactoring Cosmos ecosystem frontend code for Injective execution paths.
projects.content2: Refactored transaction submission to align with Injective SDK, improved execution diagnostics through Base64 message decoding, and added local caching for contract-heavy workflows.
projects.content3: Contributed protocol data and adapters to Cosmos Chain Registry, Injective Labs, Keplr Contract Registry, and DeFi Llama.
projects.stack: TypeScript, React, Keplr, Injective SDK, Caching
scope: Ported and refactored multisig frontend infrastructure to Injective chain paths
Handled CosmWasm contract interfaces and Keplr wallet integrations
Implemented transaction diagnostics and decoding
Contributed protocol data and adapters to core registries
context: CW3 fixed multisig dApps lacked robust Injective support and chain-specific transaction serialization.
Refactoring was needed to bring this infrastructure to Injective.
problemSpace: Incompatible transaction paths with standard Cosmos SDK chains
Hard-to-debug transaction failures with encoded base64 contract messages
Caching and performance issues for heavy contract query workloads
approach: Refactor submission pipeline to utilize the Injective SDK
Add Base64 decoding for in-browser message verification
Leverage query-level caching to avoid rate-limiting on node queries
outcomes: Delivered functional CW3 multisig interface for Injective users
Contributed vital chain registration adapters back to Keystone/Keplr registries and DeFi Llama

projects.title: Cryptech Developments Ltd.
projects.content: Built and maintained the Cryptech web platform and frontend systems for associated product surfaces, including Macro DEX interface work.
projects.content2: Focused on reusable frontend implementation in React/Next.js and component-level consistency across company and product properties.
scope: Built company landing pages and developer platform dashboard
Maintained reusable CSS style guide and UI component library
Integrated Macro DEX exchange interfaces
context: Cryptech Developments required a centralized platform dashboard and corporate website that shared components and brand styling.
problemSpace: Component fragmentation and styling drift between company marketing sites and the developer dashboard
Responsive alignment and asset packaging for low-latency dashboard operations
approach: Create shared Tailwind configurations and custom CSS utility layer
Wrap interactive states into clean, reusable React components
outcomes: Unified developer dashboard and platform sites with shared style guidelines
Reduced component development time for new features by implementing design tokens

projects.title: All About Blockchain
projects.content: Co-founded All About Blockchain, focused on node infrastructure and ecosystem product development.
projects.content2: Contributed to Stakefolio, a staking portfolio tracking application with wallet-linked data views and multi-network portfolio context.
projects.stack: TypeScript, React, Next.js, Figma, Tailwind, Vercel
scope: Co-founded node validation infrastructure company
Designed and built Stakefolio, a multi-network staking tracker
Integrated wallet interactions and aggregated network telemetry
context: All About Blockchain was founded to operate secure validator infrastructure while delivering simple interfaces for staking portfolio management.
problemSpace: Users delegating tokens across multiple networks lack a unified visual overview of their rewards and status
Maintaining correct portfolio state across different RPC interfaces (Cosmos, Injective) is highly complex
approach: Build a multi-chain dashboard using Next.js to aggregate staking indices
Design visual dashboard layouts directly in Figma and port them to Tailwind utility classes
outcomes: Successfully launched node operations on Cosmos and Injective ecosystems
Delivered Stakefolio tracking tools for managing multi-chain delegation profiles

projects.title: Cosmoverse 2023 / Hackathons
projects.content: Built a dynamic event scheduling interface for Cosmoverse 2023 with externalized schedule data for fast operational updates.
projects.content2: Contributed to hackathon systems including Dyve (NFT lending/renting/shorting primitive) and Turing.Bet (wallet-connected prediction workflow).
projects.content3: Satsuma implemented a cross-chain yield routing system: USDC flow on Injective, yield skimming and swap through Astroport, bridge handoff via Axelar GMP, and Bitcoin conversion orchestration on ICP.

ctaPanelOverrides.description: Case studies & shipping scope: cross-chain yield routing UI (Injective, Astroport, Axelar GMP, ICP).

projects.stack: React, TypeScript, Astroport, Axelar GMP, Injective, ICP
scope: Designed and built the user interface for Satsuma yield routing
Handled multi-chain wallet status and status transitions
Implemented yield accounting calculations and route visualization
context: Built during the Cosmoverse hackathon to automate the conversion of yield to Bitcoin across multiple chains.
Shipped at Cosmoverse hackathon operating in a fully agentic engineering flow, directing implementation through chat while maintaining product and design ownership throughout.
problemSpace: High friction in cross-chain UX due to slow state handoffs between Injective and ICP
Yield accounting complexity across multiple pools and protocols
Wallet state syncing across multiple networks simultaneously
approach: Model cross-chain transition stages as an animated, step-by-step progress tracker
Aggregate Astroport pool rates and ICP canister states on the client side
outcomes: Shipped the full working prototype under a fast hackathon schedule
Demonstrated cross-chain yield routing with Astroport and Axelar GMP integrations

projects.title: Personal Projects
projects.content: Independent web systems delivered outside DeFi, including marketplace and business websites with production deployments and ongoing maintenance.
projects.content2: Representative work includes World Antiques and Collectables and Dungarvan Creek.
scope: Built and maintained client commercial websites
Designed SEO-optimized landing structures and CMS content pipes
Maintained continuous deployment cycles via Vercel
context: Delivered web systems for local businesses, focusing on rapid page loads, clean responsive design, and solid contact conversions.
problemSpace: Small business websites need very fast page speed and intuitive navigation to minimize bounce rates
Low-maintenance content management solutions for non-technical administrators
approach: Leverage Next.js static page generation and dynamic routing
Optimize images and load paths, ensuring high core web vitals
outcomes: Deployed and maintained production applications for World Antiques and Dungarvan Creek
Substantially increased search engine visibility and user conversions via semantic layout and custom styling
